questions.question: Which of the following best defines cloud computing?

options.option: On-demand delivery of computing resources over the internet with pay-as-you-go pricing
options.comment: Cloud computing is on-demand delivery of IT resources with flexible pricing models.

options.option: A local network of computers connected via cables
Storage devices connected to the internet
Weather data services provided over the internet

questions.question: What does elasticity mean in cloud computing?

options.option: The ability to automatically scale resources up or down based on demand
options.comment: Elasticity allows systems to handle traffic spikes and reduce costs during low demand.

options.option: The ability to physically stretch cables across regions
The flexibility to use rubber materials for hardware
The speed at which data travels over the network

questions.question: What does IaaS stand for, and what does the provider manage in this model?

options.option: Infrastructure as a Service; provider manages hardware, virtualization, and networking
options.comment: IaaS customers manage OS, middleware, runtime, and applications. Providers manage everything below.

options.option: Internet and Application Service; provider manages user data
Integrated Application Stack; provider manages everything except hardware
Internet-based Authentication System; provider manages user logins

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of PaaS (Platform as a Service)?

options.option: Azure App Service or Heroku for deploying web applications
options.comment: PaaS provides a managed platform with runtime, frameworks, and deployment tools pre-configured.

options.option: Amazon EC2 virtual machines
Microsoft Office 365 cloud apps
Google Drive file storage

questions.question: What is SaaS, and what is a common characteristic?

options.option: Software as a Service; accessed via web browser, fully managed by provider
options.comment: SaaS is ready-to-use software hosted in the cloud (Salesforce, Teams, Slack, etc.). Users don't manage infrastructure.

options.option: Storage as a Service; for storing only backups
Server as a Service; you must manage operating systems
Security as a Service; only for password management

questions.question: Which statement best describes a public cloud?

options.option: Multi-tenant infrastructure shared by many organizations, accessible over the internet
options.comment: Public clouds (AWS, Azure, GCP) serve multiple customers with shared resources and global accessibility.

options.option: Private infrastructure owned and operated by a single organization
Cloud services available only to government agencies
Cloud infrastructure restricted to a specific country or region

questions.question: When would a company choose a private cloud over a public cloud?

options.option: When they need maximum control, compliance requirements, and data sovereignty
options.comment: Private clouds are ideal for industries with strict regulations (healthcare, finance) and sensitive data requirements.

options.option: When they want to save the most money
When they need to use only open-source software
When they want the fastest possible internet speeds

questions.question: What is the main advantage of a hybrid cloud approach?

options.option: Flexibility to keep sensitive workloads private while using public cloud for non-critical tasks
options.comment: Hybrid clouds balance security, cost, and flexibility by using both private and public resources strategically.

options.option: It eliminates the need for Internet connectivity
It doubles the storage capacity automatically
It guarantees 100% uptime across all services

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a Virtual Network (VNet) in cloud computing?

options.option: To create an isolated network environment for cloud resources with custom IP ranges and subnets
options.comment: VNets (Virtual Networks) allow you to segment resources, control routing, and implement security boundaries.

options.option: To connect only to the internet backbone
To provide unlimited storage space
To automatically encrypt all data

questions.question: What does a Security Group do in cloud networking?

options.option: Acts as a firewall to control inbound and outbound traffic to cloud resources
options.comment: Security Groups are stateful firewalls that allow/deny traffic based on rules you define.

options.option: Encrypts all data stored on the hard drive
Monitors employee activity and generates reports
Creates physical barriers around data centers

questions.question: What type of data is object storage best suited for?

options.option: Unstructured data like photos, videos, documents, and backups
options.comment: Object storage is designed for massive amounts of unstructured data accessed via HTTP APIs.

options.option: Only structured data in SQL tables
Real-time transactional databases only
Operating system files exclusively

questions.question: What is a key characteristic of block storage?

options.option: Attached to VMs as virtual disks, providing low-latency storage for OS and databases
options.comment: Block storage provides persistent volume storage with high IOPS for transactional workloads.

options.option: Stored only in memory and is temporary
Can only be accessed over the internet
Is always encrypted at the hardware level automatically

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Identity and Access Management (IAM) in the cloud?

options.option: To control who has access to cloud resources and what actions they can perform
options.comment: IAM enables fine-grained access control through users, roles, policies, and permissions.

options.option: To encrypt all data in the cloud
To monitor internet speeds only
To automatically delete unused resources

questions.question: What does the 'Principle of Least Privilege' mean in cloud security?

options.option: Grant users only the minimum permissions needed to perform their job
options.comment: Least Privilege minimizes risk by ensuring users have just enough access, reducing attack surface.

options.option: Give all users full administrator access for convenience
Restrict cloud access only to C-level executives
Remove all passwords and use only biometric authentication

questions.question: In the cloud's shared responsibility model, who is responsible for securing the hypervisor and physical infrastructure?

options.option: The cloud provider
options.comment: Providers secure the underlying infrastructure; customers secure their data, applications, and configurations.

options.option: The customer organization
Both equally share the responsibility
A third-party security firm is always responsible

questions.question: Which of the following is an effective cost optimization strategy in the cloud?

options.option: Right-sizing resources, using reserved instances, and removing unused resources
options.comment: Cost optimization includes matching resource size to actual needs and committing to reserved capacity for predictable workloads.

options.option: Purchasing the largest VM available
Storing all backups in the most expensive tier
Running all workloads in multiple regions simultaneously

questions.question: What are spot instances, and when should they be used?

options.option: Discounted unused capacity, ideal for fault-tolerant and flexible workloads like batch processing
options.comment: Spot instances offer up to 90% savings but can be interrupted. Perfect for non-critical tasks.

options.option: Premium instances with guaranteed uptime for critical workloads
Instances that are always stored in the same physical location
Only available during nighttime hours

questions.question: What is the purpose of cloud governance?

options.option: To ensure resources are used safely, efficiently, and in compliance with policies
options.comment: Governance includes policies, controls, auditing, and tagging to maintain security and compliance standards.

options.option: To limit cloud adoption within organizations
To eliminate the need for security measures
To monitor only internet traffic speeds

questions.question: Why is resource tagging important in cloud governance?

options.option: It enables tracking of ownership, environment, cost allocation, and compliance status of resources
options.comment: Tags organize resources for billing, compliance audits, automated management, and policy enforcement.

options.option: It automatically encrypts all resources
It increases the storage capacity of the cloud
It restricts all external internet access
